_meta.title: Kenya Competency-Based Education (CBE) Curriculum Reference
_meta.source: Compiled from Kenya Institute of Curriculum Development (KICD) curriculum designs (kicd.ac.ke) and related KICD-derived public documents
_meta.note: This is a REPRESENTATIVE SAMPLE, not the complete official curriculum. CBE spans PP1 to Grade 12 with dozens of learning areas, each containing many strands, sub-strands, and detailed specific learning outcomes, key inquiry questions, suggested learning experiences, PCIs, values, and assessment rubrics for every sub-strand. This file selects representative learning areas per level/grade and 1-2 verified or closely-paraphrased specific learning outcomes per sub-strand. For authoritative, complete, and current curriculum designs, always refer directly to https://kicd.ac.ke/cbc-materials/curriculum-designs/

structure_terms.grade: A year/level of schooling (replaces 'class' or 'standard' from the old 8-4-4 system)
structure_terms.strand: A broad topic area within a learning area (replaces 'topic')
structure_terms.sub_strand: A smaller component within a strand (replaces 'sub-topic')
structure_terms.specific_learning_outcomes: What the learner should know, do, or value by the end of the sub-strand (replaces 'objectives')

outcomes: Develop appropriate listening skills from varied experiences to enrich their ability to communicate
Participate in conversation using appropriate verbal and non-verbal language in everyday experiences
Express own opinions, ideas and feelings creatively, freely and confidently in varied situations as they appreciate others
Sort and classify objects according to one attribute such as colour, shape or size
Match objects in a one-to-one correspondence for counting readiness
Communicate appropriately using verbal and/or non-verbal modes in a variety of contexts
Demonstrate appropriate etiquette in social relationships
Recite number names in sequence for number readiness
Demonstrate basic numeracy skills for learning
Create artworks to share ideas, thoughts, feelings and experiences for learning and enjoyment
Appreciate own and others' artworks from within their social context and culture
Sort objects according to colour, shape and size for classification
Make patterns using real objects to develop logical reasoning
Recite number names up to 50 in sequence
Identify place value of ones and tens
Name the parts of the body that are in use when walking for body awareness
Perform walking in different ways for strength, coordination, endurance, balance and space awareness
Perform running in different ways for strength, coordination, endurance, balance and space awareness
Observe rules when playing games for own and others' safety
Perform jumping for height in different ways for strength, coordination, endurance, balance and space awareness
Appreciate jumping for height for strength, coordination, balance and self-esteem
Perform stretching in different ways for flexibility, agility, coordination and space awareness
Establish relationships through stretching for critical thinking and problem solving
Perform the overarm throw in different ways for strength, coordination, endurance, balance and space awareness
Make appropriate play items for creativity and imagination
Familiarize with the pool rules for safety
Observe sanitation around the pool environs for personal hygiene

sub_strands.sub_strand: Basic Swimming Skills: Exhaling in Water
outcomes: Practice exhaling in water for sustained swimming
Appreciate exhaling in water for stamina and endurance
Perform the single leg balance for coordination, balance, strength and space awareness
Appreciate performing the single leg balance for self-esteem
Name ways of keeping the school environment clean
Clean the school environment for the well-being of self and others
Mention differences between family needs and wants
Classify family needs and wants for financial literacy awareness

sub_strands.sub_strand: Care of Personal and Common Items
outcomes: Differentiate between personal and common items used at home
Clean personal and common items using locally available materials
Identify the place value of ones, tens and hundreds
Use place value to compare and order numbers
Kutumia lugha ya heshima katika mazungumzo ya kila siku
Kushiriki katika mazungumzo kwa kujiamini
Practice hygiene, nutrition, sanitation and safety skills to promote health and wellbeing
Demonstrate correct handwashing procedure to prevent communicable diseases

sub_strands.sub_strand: Properties of the Three States of Matter
outcomes: Demonstrate the properties of the three states of matter through hands-on activities
Practice preventive health measures, such as avoiding blowing air into a shared balloon, when carrying out activities on matter
Identify solid waste within the school compound and its environment
Discuss ways of managing different types of solid waste in the environment
Explain the meaning of water conservation
Discuss ways water is wasted at home and at school

sub_strands.sub_strand: Location, Position and Size of Kenya
outcomes: Identify countries that neighbour Kenya
Locate the position of Kenya in relation to her neighbours using a compass direction
Value good relations between Kenya and her neighbours
Recognise sounds, words and phrases correctly
Listen for the main idea and specific details for effective communication
Record specific details in a variety of forms
Acknowledge the importance of filling forms correctly
Read and write numbers in symbols and words up to millions
Round off numbers to the nearest given place value
Classify plants and animals based on observable characteristics
Appreciate the role of plants and animals in the ecosystem
Identify place value and total value of digits in large numbers
Apply whole numbers to perform mathematical operations in real-life situations
Listen attentively and respond to a variety of oral texts
Use appropriate non-verbal cues during listening for effective communication
Identify common crops grown in the local area
Practice basic crop husbandry activities such as planting and tending crops in the school farm
Explain the meaning and importance of entrepreneurship
Design a simple mini business plan and pitch the idea to peers

sub_strands.sub_strand: Decision Making and Coping with Stress
outcomes: Identify personal strengths and areas for self-improvement
Demonstrate appropriate ways of coping with stress in day-to-day life
Distinguish between an element and a compound
Relate common elements to their chemical symbols
Appreciate the information found on packaging labels of commonly consumed substances
Identify print and non-print texts that are interesting to read
Appreciate the importance of reading for enjoyment
Use polite expressions appropriately during telephone conversations
Demonstrate conversational skills such as disagreeing politely
Describe personality traits for self-understanding
Appreciate the role of values in the development of personality traits
Apply the scientific method to investigate a problem
Appreciate the role of scientific investigation in solving real-life problems
Select appropriate methods of separating different types of mixtures
Apply separation techniques to solve everyday problems
Describe the interdependence between living things and their environment
Advocate for conservation of the environment for sustainable living
Identify various forms and sources of energy used in day-to-day life
Appreciate the importance of renewable sources of energy for sustainable development
Identify and categorise whole numbers as odd, even, prime, composite, rational and irrational
Use the reciprocal of real numbers to work out mathematical tasks
Derive and apply the laws of indices in different situations
Determine and apply common logarithms of numbers in mathematical computations

sub_strands.sub_strand: Formation and Simplification of Quadratic Expressions
outcomes: Form quadratic expressions from given mathematical statements or real-life situations
Simplify quadratic expressions using appropriate mathematical operations

grades.pathway_note: Senior School learners take 4 mandatory subjects (English, Kiswahili/KSL, Physical Education, Community Service Learning) plus a Mathematics requirement, and choose elective learning areas from one of three pathways: STEM, Social Sciences, and Arts & Sports Science.

outcomes: Explain the basic principles of Mendelian inheritance
Apply genetic principles to solve simple monohybrid cross problems
Explain the principles guiding Kenya's foreign policy
Appreciate Kenya's role in regional and international cooperation

grades.pathway_note: Subjects and outcomes vary by the pathway and track (e.g., Pure Sciences, Applied Sciences, Technical Studies under STEM; Languages, Humanities and Business Studies under Social Sciences; Sports Science, Arts under Arts & Sports Science) selected at the end of Grade 9.

outcomes: Plan and execute a community service project addressing an identified community need
Present a portfolio of evidence demonstrating impact and reflection on the project

grades.pathway_note: Grade 12 concludes Senior School with the Kenya Senior School Education Assessment (KSSEA), which examines English, Kiswahili, Mathematics, Community Service Learning, and the three pathway elective subjects.
